title: Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the High Council of the [[New Jedi Order]]|[[High Council (disambiguation)|other uses of the word]]}} {{MultipleIssues|image}} {{NPOV}} {{Organization |image=[[File:Legacy_-25_council.jpg]] |name=Jedi High Council |type=[[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' |founder=[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] |leader=*[[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]''[[Fate of the Jedi: Vortex]]'' *[[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]]''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]'' |subgroup= |headquarters=*[[Jedi Council Chamber/Legends|High Council Chamber]], [[New Jedi Temple]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]'' *[[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Ossus Academy]] *[[Hidden Temple]][[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] |locations=*[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] *[[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] *[[Taivas]] |formed=[[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] |founded=[[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]] |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] **[[Office of the Chief of State]] *[[New Jedi Order]] **[[Jedi Coalition]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'' }} {{Quote|As Jedi, either we're players in GA politics, or we're another instrument of the elected leadership, like the fleet. If we're the latter, then we might have our opinions, but we do as the legitimate leadership directs. If we're not, then we've got no more right to start interfering with the status quo than the Monster Raving Anarchist Party.|[[Kyp Durron]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''}} The '''Jedi High Council''', formerly known as the '''Masters' Council''', was the leading body of the [[New Jedi Order]] founded by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. As of [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], the Masters' Council had formed as an informal body responsible for the leadership of the Order. However, at the conclusion of the [[Swarm War]], [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] Skywalker formed an official High Council, from the loose collection of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]], that mirrored the Council of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Both the Masters' Council and its successor consisted of the Order's twelve wisest and most powerful Masters. ==History of the High Council== ===Masters' Council=== During the early years of the reformed Jedi Order, Master [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] had refrained from creating a High Council and from basing their operations on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]; with knowledge of past corruption, Skywalker hoped he could avoid creating a body that would be the puppet of the galactic government. However during the [[Dark Nest Crisis]] a schism threatened to rip apart the fledgling Order as infighting prevented any decisions from being made and unruly [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] began to do things that did not benefit the Order's public standing.''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]'' Forced to take control, Skywalker declared himself [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] at a [[Conclave on Ossus|conclave]] within the [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Academy]] and ordered any Jedi who could not put the Order first to resign. To assist him in his leadership position, Skywalker created the twelve member council that mirrored its predecessor from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]]. Unlike the old Council however, the body was not as formal and did not take absolute control of the Order for the first few years. Convening in chambers on Ossus and within the [[New Jedi Temple|Temple]] on Coruscant, the Council met with the two [[Joiner]] Knights sent by [[Raynar Thul|UnuThul]] to persuade the Masters' Council to assist the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] in a border conflict with the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. Uncovering the existence of the [[Gorog|Dark Nest]], [[Gorog]], which was manipulating the conflict without the knowledge of UnuThul and the rest of the Colony, the Council dispatched Grand Master Skywalker, and Masters [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Skywalker]] and [[Saba Sebatyne]] to destroy Gorog. Aided by Knight [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Solo]] and several other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], the Masters ended the conflict. ===Jedi High Council=== Following the events of the [[Swarm War]] and the way it was instigated by rogue Knights, Grand Master Skywalker decided that a more authoritative High Council was necessary. Taking the [[Jedi Council Chamber/Legends|formal Council chamber]] on Coruscant as the permanent meeting place of the group, the Masters' Council was formalized into a High Council and began forming its close ties with the [[Office of the Chief of State]].{{Blog|Holocron|32|Jedi High Council Roster (NJO and beyond)}} Making decisions on behalf of the Order, the number of Knights during this time flourished and began to grow to numbers comparative to those before the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Recovering lost information and texts, the Council drew on the teachings of the old Order to better the curriculum at the academy. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], after the [[Telos Holocron]] was recovered, many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] feared that there may be attempts to steal it. Calling it to a vote, nearly half the Council suggested destroying it; though the motion was struck down and the artifact locked away in the Temple vaults.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] ====Second Galactic Civil War==== [[File:PurgeSecondsToDieNJOVision.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Several members of the High Council, as seen in [[Sha Koon]]'s [[Force vision/Legends|vision]].]] When the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] erupted, the High Council was largely ineffective, as it failed to prevent the fall of [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Catering to Grand Master Skywalker's wishes, the Council was constantly rendered inert as it clashed with Chief [[Cal Omas]] and each other. With [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady]] [[Lumiya]] orchestrating events, [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alema Rar]] on the loose, and a conflicted membership, the Council was unable to prevent the death of countless Alliance personnel, citizens, and two of their own: Masters [[Tresina Lobi]] and Mara Skywalker. With the Grand Master's wife dead and his son under the pall of the corrupt Colonel Solo, the Council failed to take action when [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] members infiltrated the Temple itself in an attempt to arrest Knight Leia Solo and her husband [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]]. When Chief Omas was assassinated and replaced by Colonel Solo himself, the Alliance government constantly harried the Order by [[Battle of Ossus (Second Galactic Civil War)|attacking]] the Ossus Academy which nearly saw the death of Council members [[Kam Solusar|Kam]] and [[Tionne Solusar]]. When Colonel Solo began to [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|burn]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the Council finally withdrew from the Alliance and went into exile on the [[Endor/Legends|Sanctuary Moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. With the help of Alliance personnel under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Cha Niathal]]'s command and the fleets of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], the newly formed [[Jedi Coalition|Coalition]] eventually brought an end to Solo's regime and took back the Alliance.''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ====Post-Second Galactic Civil War==== Following that conflict, the Order helped install former Admiral [[Natasi Daala]] as Chief of the Alliance and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jagged Fel]] as [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During Chief Daala's tenure, she ordered the arrest of Grand Master Skywalker on the charges of dereliction of duty for not preventing Colonel Solo's rampage. Instead of being imprisoned, Skywalker negotiated a deal with Daala; Skywalker would go into exile, cutting off all ties with the Order until he could discover what had cause Solo's mania. In his stead, Council member [[Kenth Hamner]] would take up the mantle of Grand Master and lead the Order until the time that Skywalker could return.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' Agreeing to the terms set forth, Daala was appeased until several Knights began to be overcome with an [[Force psychosis|enigmatic psychosis]]. Deeming them a threat to civilization, Daala ordered their arrest and began a campaign against the Jedi to recover the supposedly insane Knights. Freezing Council member [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn's]] two Jedi children in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]], Daala continued to stand unopposed by the Council, as Grand Master Hamner encouraged patience. When Daala instigated a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Siege of the New Jedi Temple|siege]] on the Temple which resulted in the cold-blooded murder of Jedi [[Kani Asari]], the High Council continued to grow impatient with Hamner's stance of inaction. When Hamner made a secret deal with Admiral [[Nek Bwua'tu]] to end the siege, the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] was attacked and fell into a coma. While waiting for him to awaken, the Knights under medical watch recovered, while a slave revolt broke out on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. With so much happening at once, Hamner became completely overwhelmed, and when the High Council learned of his secret agreement with Bwua'tu which had kept them trapped in the Temple, the High Council dismissed him as Grand Master and ordered him to his chambers for confinement. Completely hysterical, Hamner attacked his guards with the dark side and hid amongst the catwalks of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Operations Hangar]] in preparation to [[Assassin/Legends|strike down]] his opposition amongst the Council. Attacking Master [[Saba Sebatyne]], Hamner engaged in a fierce duel, eventually impaling the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Too late to awe into submission the other Jedi in the hangar who were preparing to launch their [[StealthX]]s to aid the imperiled Master Skywalker, Hamner attempted to take out the hangar doors but was stopped by Sebatyne, who defeated the former Grand Master by blasting him off a catwalk with the Force. As she tried to stop his lightsaber strike on the hangar doors telekinetically, Sebatyne was forced to release her Force grasp holding Hamner aloft, who then plummeted to his death several meters below. Following Hamner's death, Sebatyne reluctantly became the Order's acting Grand Master. ====Sith-Imperial War and Second Imperial Civil War==== Nearly a century later, the High Council continued to preside over the Order's affairs, with Grand Master Skywalker's descendant, [[Kol Skywalker]], as its leader. However, with the [[Fel Empire|Imperial]] victory during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] and the subsequent [[Massacre at Ossus]], the Jedi Order was decimated, and the High Council scattered. Fleeing extermination, three Masters reconvened the august body within the halls of the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] of [[Taivas]]. Made up of three Masters, [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], [[Tili Qua]] and [[K'Kruhk]], the Council was dedicated to rebuilding the Order. When an Imperial delegation of the Fel Empire discovered the Temple's location, the Council received them to discuss an alliance. During the same meeting the Council listened to [[Cade Skywalker]]'s plans to assassinate the Emperor and Sith Lord [[Darth Krayt]]. After meditating on the consequences of joining with [[Roan Fel]] and wiping out the Sith leadership, the Council decided that only the former option was the best way to proceed. Arguing that killing Krayt would only cause more dangerous factions to arise, the Council was more determined to wait patiently as the Order grew strong enough to strike at the Sith. After the apparent death of Darth Krayt the Jedi Council felt it was time again to take part in the struggle against the Sith. To this end they sent [[Te Corso]], [[Sayar Dun'La]], [[Asaak Dan]], and [[Drok]] to meet with Admiral [[Gar Stazi]] and form an alliance with him.[[Legacy (2006) 42|''Legacy'' (2006) 42]] ==Organization and politics== Unlike the Council of the Old Order, originally Grand Master Skywalker intended it to serve as an advisory body that would assist in guiding Knights to where they needed to go. However, this structure failed when wayward Knights nearly dragged the Order and the Alliance into all out war with the Chiss Ascendancy on behalf of the Killik Nests. After some reform the High Council became the unquestioned leader of the Order, assigning missions and expecting reports afterward. Like the old Council, this body worked closely with the executive branch of the galactic regime, advising and assisting the Chief of State of the Alliance on important diplomatic matters. Membership on this august body was limited to twelve Masters at a time, with each member appointed by unanimous vote of the current members. It was not unheard of that seats would remain unfilled for an extended period of time; following the deaths of [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] and [[Tresina Lobi]], their seats remained vacant for years. With the death of [[Kenth Hamner]] in [[44 ABY]], the Council had only nine sitting members. Convening with the austere [[Jedi Council Chamber/Legends|High Council Chamber]] in the Coruscant Temple, the Masters elected one of their own to serve as secretary during the early years after the Swarm War.''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' Not infrequently, the High Council would request the presence of any available Master, bringing in extra chairs to suit the needs of those in attendance. The relationship between the High Council and the Alliance government was often tense, and a matter of contention within the Council. Some Masters, such as [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] and Kenth Hamner, believed the Order was primarily responsible to serve the needs of the Alliance, which funded the Order during, including the reconstruction of the Temple following the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Others, including Mara Skywalker and [[Kyp Durron]], believed that giving preference to the Alliance's interest made the Jedi nothing more than mercenaries, and that the Jedi should preserve peace without favoritism, even at the expense of the Alliance. Despite their official stance, the Galactic Alliance bureaucracy came to see the Jedi as their own private diplomatic corps and police force. Both sides were convinced that they were in the right, with some arguments coming close to being decided at saber-point, and it was only Master Skywalker's moral authority that kept the Council from splitting violently. Once he left into exile, even this was not enough, as Kenth Hamner, deposed by his peers for his deception of them and support of the Galactic Alliance, revolted against the New Jedi Order, subduing his minders and the StealthX guards with deceit, physical combat, and [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. Hamner then sought to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Saba Sebatyne in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] and reclaim his power, before shifting goals to leaving the Jedi stranded and exposed to the Galactic Alliance's forces. The later was obliged to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him in order to prevent him from stopping the launch of the ships to aid Luke Skywalker. ==Members== {|class="wikitable" |- !colspan="2"|'''High Council Member''' !Succeeded !Tenure !Notes |- |[[File:Cilghalcolor.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Cilghal]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]]– | |- |[[File:Kyp_Durron_Infobox.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Kyp Durron]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY– |- |[[File:KenthHamner-Vortex.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Kenth Hamner]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY–[[44 ABY]] |Killed whilst a councilmember by [[Saba Sebatyne]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |- |[[File:Corran_Horn_by_Brian_Rood.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY– |- |[[File:OctaKyle.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Kyle Katarn]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY– |- |[[File:Njo_tresinalobi.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Tresina Lobi]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY–[[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'' |Killed whilst a councilmember by [[Lumiya]] and [[Alema Rar]] on Coruscant |- |[[File:Njo_octaramis.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Octa Ramis]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY– |- |[[File:Saba_Sebatyne_by_Brian_Rood.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Saba Sebatyne]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY– |- |[[File:Luke_Skywalker_EA.jpg|125px]] |style="text-align:center;"| [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] |style="text-align:center;"| - |style="text-align:center;"| 35 ABY–[[43 ABY]]
